response:
## Verdict: 2 block · 11 warn · 9 ignore

Categorisation logic: **block** if a finding is either high-severity *or* a real defect that ships broken behaviour an agent can't recover from. **Warn** for medium-severity findings — agent_risk in the 0.5–0.6 band means future edits are likely to compound the mess, but nothing is outright wrong today. **Ignore** for low-severity stylistic / hygiene findings that don't shift the risk profile of this PR.

### Block merge (2)

| ID | Charge | sev / agent_risk | Why block |
|----|--------|------------------|-----------|
| crime_00001 | God Function (`generateInvoice`, 204 lines, 3.4× threshold) | **high** / 0.63 | Only `high` finding in the report. 204-line domain function ships unreviewable code; any subsequent agent edit will miss interactions. Should be split before this lands. |
| crime_00006 | Test That Proves Nothing (`"renders the enterprise plan"`, 0 expects) | medium 0.45 / 0.46 | Scores are mid, but this test is the named coverage for the God Function above — it actively creates false confidence. A no-op test in the same PR as the function it claims to cover is a defect, not a smell. Delete or assert. |

### Warn (11)

All medium severity, agent_risk clustered 0.50–0.61. Author should address or explicitly acknowledge before merge, but none independently breaks the build:

| ID | Charge | sev / agent_risk | Note |
|----|--------|------------------|------|
| crime_00002 | Missing Agent Context | 0.6 / 0.61 | High agent_risk by construction — repo ships zero agent instructions. Fixable in one file. |
| crime_00004 | Temporal Recklessness (`billing.ts`, 7 calls) | 0.6 / 0.53 | Domain code reading wall clock; couples future tests to time. |
| crime_00005 | Temporal Recklessness (`date.ts`, 5 calls) | 0.6 / 0.61 | Same pattern, test_gap=1 pushes agent_risk higher. |
| crime_00007 | String Sprinkles (`"enterprise"` across 4 areas) | 0.5 / 0.60 | Policy literal spread across api/billing/jobs/ui — duplicated policy in waiting. |
| crime_00008 | False Identity (`calculateRefundPreview` does side effects) | 0.55 / 0.52 | Borderline block — name lies about purity. Warn because callers in this PR are limited. |
| crime_00009 | Concept Alias Drift (`tenant`/account/team/workspace/org) | 0.55 / 0.58 | Vocabulary fork; pick canonical term before adding more routes. |
| crime_00010 | God File (`billing.ts`, 310 lines) | 0.6 / 0.50 | Co-occurs with crime_00001 — splitting the function likely resolves both. |
| crime_00011 | Logic in the Alibi (refund rule in comment only) | 0.5 / 0.50 | Real business rule hiding in a comment; encode as guard or test. |
| crime_00012 | Duplicated Navigation Source (`/settings/billing` → "Plans" vs "Billing") | 0.6 / 0.57 | Borderline block — users will see inconsistent labels. Warn because cosmetic, not load-bearing. |
| crime_00013 | Unfinished Business (14 markers, 424/1k LOC in `todo.ts`) | 0.4 / 0.57 | Pathological TODO density — agents can't tell intentional behaviour from placeholders. |
| crime_00014 | Route Metadata Drift (`/settings/billing` named 4 ways) | 0.6 / 0.56 | Same root cause cluster as crime_00012; fix together. |

### Ignore (9)

All low severity. agent_risk ≤ 0.51 across the board, mostly hygiene noise that doesn't justify blocking this PR:

| ID | Charge | sev / agent_risk | Why ignore |
|----|--------|------------------|------------|
| crime_00015 | Docs-Code Drift (`./setup.md` missing) | 0.35 / 0.51 | One stale doc link — fix in follow-up. |
| crime_00016 | Test That Proves Nothing (weak matcher) | 0.25 / 0.34 | Weak but not empty assertion; far lower priority than crime_00006. |
| crime_00017 | Option Bag Junk Drawer | 0.3 / 0.48 | Local to `petty.ts`; no blast radius. |
| crime_00018 | Return Shape Roulette | 0.3 / 0.48 | Same file, same scope. |
| crime_00019 | Negative Flag Maze | 0.25 / 0.47 | Two-flag condition; readable enough today. |
| crime_00020 | Unfinished Business (`billing.ts`, 4 markers) | 0.2 / 0.40 | Just above the 10/1k threshold; folded into the billing.ts cleanup. |
| crime_00021 | Orphaned Destination `/account/subscription` | 0.4 / 0.46 | Route exists but unreachable — likely in-flight. Ignore unless author confirms it's dead. |
| crime_00022 | Orphaned Destination `/team` | 0.4 / 0.46 | Same. |
| crime_00023 | Orphaned Destination `/workspace/members` | 0.4 / 0.46 | Same. |

### Notes for the author

- **crimes_00001 + 00006 + 00010** are one cluster: split `generateInvoice`, write real assertions, the file shrinks naturally.
- **crime_00009 + 00021/00022/00023** are one cluster: tenant/team/workspace/account naming fork shows up in code *and* the orphaned routes — settle the canonical concept before wiring those routes into nav.
- **crime_00012 + 00014** are one cluster: pick one name for `/settings/billing` and align nav + route metadata + page title in a single change.
